symptoms: Insufficient privileges to complete the operation
MissingApplicationAdministrator.cause: The signed-in user does not have an Entra role that can approve the required application permissions.
MissingApplicationAdministrator.fix: Ask a Global Administrator, Cloud Application Administrator, or Application Administrator to complete consent.

symptoms: Bicep is not available on PATH
BicepMissing.cause: The local machine cannot run Bicep deployments yet.
BicepMissing.fix: Install Azure CLI with Bicep support or add Bicep to PATH before running deployment.

symptoms: The signed-in Azure tenant does not match the customer package
AzureTenantMismatch.cause: The installer is authenticated to a different tenant than the one in the customer install package.
AzureTenantMismatch.fix: Sign in to the correct customer tenant or load a package generated for the current tenant.

symptoms: Microsoft Graph sign-in is required for Entra permission checks
GraphNotSignedIn.cause: The installer cannot validate Entra consent readiness without a Microsoft Graph session.
GraphNotSignedIn.fix: Sign in to Microsoft Graph with the required scopes before running Entra permission checks.

symptoms: Microsoft Graph sign-in is required for SharePoint access checks
GraphNotSignedInForSharePoint.cause: The installer cannot resolve the SharePoint site through Graph without a signed-in Graph session.
GraphNotSignedInForSharePoint.fix: Sign in to Microsoft Graph with SharePoint site read permissions, then rerun preflight.

AzureSignInFailed.cause: The user cancelled sign-in, used the wrong tenant account, or does not have access to the target subscription.
AzureSignInFailed.fix: Retry Azure sign-in with an account that belongs to the customer tenant and has access to the target subscription.

symptoms: Microsoft Graph sign-in did not complete
GraphSignInFailed.cause: The user cancelled sign-in or did not approve the requested Microsoft Graph scopes.
GraphSignInFailed.fix: Retry Microsoft Graph sign-in and approve the required tenant, app consent, and SharePoint scopes.

symptoms: Azure RBAC may not allow deployment
AzureRbacInsufficient.cause: The signed-in account does not appear to have Owner or Contributor access at the subscription or resource group scope.
AzureRbacInsufficient.fix: Grant Contributor or Owner access for the target resource group or subscription, then rerun preflight.

symptoms: Additional Graph consent scopes may be required
GraphConsentScopesMissing.cause: The current Microsoft Graph session does not include every scope needed for app consent and SharePoint validation.
GraphConsentScopesMissing.fix: Sign in to Microsoft Graph again and approve all requested scopes.

symptoms: The signed-in Graph account may not be able to approve app consent
EntraAdminRoleMissing.cause: The signed-in account is not an active Global Administrator, Cloud Application Administrator, or Application Administrator.
EntraAdminRoleMissing.fix: Use an account with one of the supported Entra admin roles for the consent step.

symptoms: SharePoint site could not be resolved through Microsoft Graph
SharePointSiteResolveFailed.cause: The site URL is wrong, the Graph tenant is wrong, or the signed-in Graph account lacks SharePoint read consent.
SharePointSiteResolveFailed.fix: Confirm the SharePoint URL, sign in to the correct Graph tenant with Sites.Read.All, then rerun preflight.
